title: vSAN Witness Appliance Health
spl:
index=hci sourcetype="vsan:witness" earliest=-24h
| where witness_state!="connected" OR quorum!="met"
| stats latest(witness_state) as ws, latest(quorum) as q by cluster
| table cluster, ws, q
description: Stretched vSAN witness availability and quorum for split-brain prevention.
value: Stretched vSAN witness availability and quorum for split-brain prevention.
implementation: Poll witness health API. Page immediately if witness disconnected or quorum lost.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: vCenter, witness VM metrics.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `vsan:witness`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll witness health API. Page immediately if witness disconnected or quorum lost.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hci sourcetype="vsan:witness" earliest=-24h
| where witness_state!="connected" OR quorum!="met"
| stats latest(witness_state) as ws, latest(quorum) as q by cluster
| table cluster, ws, q
```

Understanding this SPL

**vSAN Witness Appliance Health** — Stretched vSAN witness availability and quorum for split-brain prevention.

Documented **Data sources**: `vsan:witness`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): vCenter, witness VM metrics. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hci; **sourcetype**: vsan:witness. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=hci, sourcetype="vsan:witness", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where witness_state!="connected" OR quorum!="met"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cluster** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Pipeline stage (see **vSAN Witness Appliance Health**): table cluster, ws, q


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (witness OK), Table (clusters at risk), Timeline.
visualization: Single value (witness OK), Table (clusters at risk), Timeline.
